channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Southwest Airlines flight abruptly rises to avoid another plane crossing Chicago runway
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Pilots on a Southwest Airlines flight attempting to land at Chicago's Midway Airport were forced to climb back into the sky to avoid another aircraft crossing the runway on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS and KATHLEEN FOODY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Death toll in Sudan military plane crash rises to 46
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The death toll from a Sudanese military aircraft crash in the city of Omdurman increased to at least 46 people, officials said Wednesday, one of the deadliest plane crashes in the northeastern African nation in the past two decades.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Americans considering spring break vacation in Mexico warned of murder spike
item.description: Spring break travelers were issued a warning from the United States' State Department about traveling to Mexico amid an uptick of murders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US and Ukraine near an economic deal with mineral rights but no security promise, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine and the U.S. have reached an agreement on a framework for a broad economic deal that would include access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals, three senior Ukrainian officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, HANNA ARHIROVA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Stress and fear roil a tiny, rapidly militarizing Japanese island near Taiwan
item.description: YONAGUNI, Japan (AP) — This tiny island on Japan's western frontier has no chain convenience stores. Nature lovers can dive with hammerhead sharks and watch miniature horses graze on a hill. But the wooded mountain ranges now carry radar sites.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The nightmare drags on for the families of hostages who remain in Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Yael Alexander has been watching the release of hostages from Gaza over the past six weeks with a mix of joy, envy and fear. Her son Edan, an American-Israeli hostage held for over 500 days, is not yet on the list of those to be freed.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Florida nurse's daughter: Hospital attack was preventable
item.description: "The fact that this happened at a healthcare facility which should have proper security is beyond me," Dr. Cindy Joseph said on "Banfield.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Things to know about the ruling blocking President Trump's refugee ban
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump's effort to suspend the system for resettling refugees in the U.S. is on hold after a federal judge in Seattle blocked it. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge OKs prison abuse settlement, rejecting Trump administration's push to rewrite protections
item.description: A judge has given final approval to a legal settlement that subjects the federal Bureau of Prisons to independent monitoring and increased transparency in the wake of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual abuse at a now-shuttered federal women’s prison in California.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US energy secretary touts nuclear power as tech sector's thirst for electricity grows
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright says it’s critical that the nation be out in front when it comes to artificial intelligence, and that means having reliable and affordable sources of electricity to meet the growing demands of the technology sector.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Young American tourists found dead at Caribbean beach resort; authorities investigating
item.description: Belize officials announced Monday that three American tourists were recently found dead at a beach resort in San Pedro. The women were between 23 and 26.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Adnan Syed's murder conviction still stands as he seeks sentence reduction in 'Serial' case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Despite documented problems with the evidence against him and an earlier request from prosecutors to clear his record, Adnan Syed will remain a convicted murderer, according to court papers filed Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he will offer 'gold cards' for $5 million path to citizenship, replacing investor visas
item.description: President Donald Trump says he plans to offer a “gold card” visa with a path to citizenship for $5 million, replacing a 35-year-old visa for investors.
item.dc:creator: Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Texas official issues warning after rancher killed by cartel IED in Mexico: 'A terrorist attack,' son says
item.description: A Texas rancher was killed by an explosive device on a road while traveling through Mexico earlier this month, officials said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missouri woman accused in plot to sell Graceland says she'll plead guilty
item.description: A Missouri woman said Tuesday she is pleading guilty to a federal charge accusing her of concocting a brazen plot to defraud Elvis Presley’s family by trying to auction off his Graceland mansion and property before a judge halted the mysterious foreclosure sale.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: “We're going to be selling a gold card. You have a green card. This is a gold card. We're going to be putting a price on that card of about $5 million and that's going to give you green card privileges,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Millions of Social Security recipients are about to get back payments
item.description: More than 3.2 million Social Security recipients who received pensions from their time as teachers, firefighters, police officers and other public service jobs will soon see a boost in their benefits.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Idaho prosecutors seek to limit Bryan Kohberger's alibi evidence at murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Idaho prosecutors say a man charged in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students shouldn't be allowed to offer an alibi defense unless he takes the stand himself during the murder trial later this year.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rare video captures super pod of 2,000 dolphins breaching and playing
item.description: A California boat captain has captured rare video of a super pod of more than 2,000 dolphins breaching off the coast of Monterey Bay, including the more elusive northern right whale dolphin.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Scenes at US Capitol on war's anniversary reflect partisan divide on Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 100 House members at one point packed the steps of the U.S. Capitol to show their support for Ukraine following Russia's invasion three years ago, Republicans joining with Democrats in a forceful display of bipartisanship.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2025 Black History Month theme recognizes labor activists
item.description: The Association for the Study of African American Life and History’s 2025 Black History Month theme is “African Americans and Labor.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suspends security clearances for Jack Smith's outside lawyers
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed a memo suspending security clearances for a number of outside lawyers who are defending former special counsel Jack Smith in his personal capacity. White House staff secretary Will Scharf said in the Oval Office that the memo targeted Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: He's had three 'last meals' on Oklahoma's death row. The Supreme Court has now tossed his conviction
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Richard Glossip has spent 27 years behind bars, most of it on Oklahoma's death row, coming close enough to execution that he has had nine separate execution dates and been fed three “last meals.” On Tuesday, Glossip, now 62, won a new trial in a stunning U.S.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah bill banning water fluoridation one step away from becoming law
item.description: Fluoridation, or water fluoridation, is the action of raising naturally occurring levels of fluoride in water systems to aid in oral health of those who drink it.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Drachman, NewsNation

item.title: Man forced to sit next to corpse after woman died midway through flight
item.description: Passenger Mitchell Ring said the flight crew did everything they could to revive the woman but they were unsuccessful.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Denver man missing at Black Canyon in Gunnison National Park
item.description: The agencies are looking for the public's help in finding him. The missing man was identified as Jordan Marsters, 31, of Denver.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Woman injured on Harry Potter theme park ride awarded $7.25 million
item.description: A federal jury has awarded $7.25 million to a 74-year-old Arizona woman for a spine injury she suffered in a fall while exiting the Harry Potter and the Forbidden Journey ride at Universal Studios Hollywood.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about the Feb. 28 Economic Blackout
item.description: Customers across the United States are looking to send a message Friday to companies that have rolled back their DEI initiatives in the form of a 24-hour consumer boycott, also known as the “Feb. 28 Economic Blackout.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, KRON4

item.title: Michigan veterinarian faces theft charge after refusing to return homeless man's dog
item.description: GRAND RAPIDS, Mich. (AP) — A Michigan veterinarian who said she rescued a distressed dog that was tied to a truck is facing a theft charge after failing to return him to a homeless man.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SWAT team raided the wrong Denver apartment and traumatized two young girls, lawsuit says
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Kirsty Shelton was at home preparing lunch and watching television with her daughters and their grandmother when they were startled by loud banging on their apartment door by a SWAT team looking for a man who lived across the hall.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WATCH: Man steals 2 bulldog puppies as another 'fakes seizure' to distract staff
item.description: Two bulldog puppies were taken from a pet store in Colorado after a man "allegedly faked a seizure to distract employees," the Arapahoe Sheriff's Office said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Massive blue city migrant shelter opens despite residents' furious opposition
item.description: A massive migrant shelter has opened in the Bronx despite residents venting their vehement opposition to the move.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FCC chairman climbs broadcast tower to promote industry, jobs
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nearly 200K sign petition to revoke Musk's Canadian citizenship
item.description: The petition calls on Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to cancel Musk's citizenship and Canadian passport.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5
